idea.title: Parry Core
idea.hook: Projectiles streak in from every edge — tap the side they're coming from to deflect them off your core. One slip and a life is gone; the swarm only gets faster.
idea.hook_ru: Снаряды летят со всех сторон — нажми сторону, откуда они летят, и отбей их от ядра. Одна ошибка — минус жизнь; рой только ускоряется.
idea.prototype: Single-screen arcade reflex. A glowing core sits at center. Threats fly inward from the 4 edges (top/right/bottom/left). The player TAPS the matching edge tap-zone (or presses arrow/WASD) to raise a shield on that side and deflect the incoming threat just before impact, inside a dashed parry ring. A deflected threat ricochets away (+score, combo multiplier). A threat that reaches the core costs a LIFE (3 lives). Threat variety for depth: fast darts (1 tap), heavy hexagons (2 taps), and splitters that fork into two adjacent lanes when parried. Speed + spawn rate ramp every wave (WAVE_THRESHOLD=8 parries/wave). LOSE when lives hit 0. Endless with persistent best score (localStorage). This is 'act, don't move' — distinct from dodging. On-screen tutorial on MENU (EN+RU) + a ? help button in HUD; a directional arrow + 'TAP this side!' prompt teaches the mechanic on the first threat.
idea.why_novel: reflex parry/deflect instead of dodge — act don't move. Daily Dodge and our other dodge games (memeDodge, dodge_run, tiny_model_dodger) all ask the player to MOVE a body away from danger. Parry Core keeps the player's core fixed and makes the verb a directional, timed PARRY of incoming threats — the player reads which of 4 edges a shard comes from and answers with a matching shield tap inside a parry window. Same proven arcade-reflex retention family, opposite input fantasy.
idea.lose_condition: gs='GAMEOVER' when lives<=0 (a threat reaching the core within CORE_RADIUS while its side's shield is not raised costs one of 3 lives).

sources.name: portfolio knowledge 2026-05-25 (BUILD-MORE: Daily Dodge)
sources.note: KNOWLEDGE pick — inside-out from our own portfolio metrics. Daily Dodge (slug daily_dodge, arcade, 73.8s/play, 17 plays, 👍4/👎0) is our single best-retaining, best-liked game and a BUILD-MORE winner. Arcade-reflex is a proven-retention family for us. Parry Core extends that winner with a fresh reflex mechanic (parry/deflect) rather than cloning the dodge mechanic, and directly addresses Daily Dodge's #1 complaint 'mobile controls are not the best' with 4 big edge tap-zones reachable by thumb in portrait.

sources.name: danger_combat CTR archetype
sources.note: CTR angle danger_combat — immediate conflict, projectile/weapon close to impact on the core, high contrast, aggressive pose. References: Age of Heroes, Super Evolution Battle, Ball Battle Simulator, Dashers.io. Cover shows the glowing core mid-impact with a bright shield flaring on one edge deflecting a shard.

synthesizer_brief: KNOWLEDGE-PICK (portfolio_knowledge origin). This extends our BUILD-MORE arcade winner Daily Dodge — our best-retaining, best-liked game at 73.8s/play with 👍4/👎0 — rather than chasing a market signal. The bet: arcade-reflex is a proven-retention family for our audience, so we mine our own winner for a SECOND mechanic in that family instead of re-shipping the dodge. The novelty is the verb: Daily Dodge (and memeDodge / dodge_run / tiny_model_dodger) are all 'move your body away from danger'; Parry Core fixes the core at center and makes the player ACT — read which of 4 edges a projectile streaks in from and answer with a timed, directional shield tap to deflect it. Three hard requirements from our own player comments are gated in: (1) CRYSTAL-CLEAR GOAL IN 5s — the first threat arrives slowly with a pulsing directional arrow + 'TAP this side!' prompt and the MENU carries a full 5-line EN+RU tutorial, so the mechanic teaches itself; (2) MOBILE-PORTRAIT-FIRST controls that fix Daily Dodge's 'mobile controls are not the best' complaint — four big diagonal edge tap-zones (every side, including the bottom thumb zone, reachable in portrait) plus arrow keys / WASD on desktop, verified at 375x667; (3) REAL DEPTH — 3+ threat types (dart / heavy / splitter on top of normal), a per-wave rising difficulty curve (faster spawns + faster shards), a combo multiplier, 3 lives, persistent best score, and escalating waves so it never feels one-note. Cover uses the danger_combat archetype: glowing core mid-impact, a bright shield flaring on one edge deflecting a shard, projectiles streaking in from all sides, high contrast.

alternatives.title: Reflect Ring
alternatives.note: A single rotating arc shield around the core that the player spins (drag/arrows) to face incoming shards — closer to a Super Hexagon / orbit-defense feel. Rejected for this slot because rotation reintroduces a 'steer a thing' input that overlaps the dodge fantasy and is harder to teach in 5s than discrete edge taps.

alternatives.title: Beat Parry
alternatives.note: Rhythm-reflex variant — threats lock to a musical beat and parries score on-beat for combo. A strong arcade-reflex direction but adds an audio/timing layer that risks the '5-second clear goal' requirement and needs sound assets (out of vanilla scope). Held as a possible iteration.

alternatives.title: Catch & Volley
alternatives.note: Instead of deflecting, the player catches a threat on the matching side and volleys it back along an aimed lane to chain-hit a spawner — a more offensive twist. Deferred: two-step input (catch then aim) is heavier than the one-tap parry and weakens the instant-readability mandate.
